description_long: This is a stone bridge featuring three prominent arches supported by thick rectangular piers. The upper surface is flat and paved, bordered by a crenellated parapet reminiscent of medieval fortifications. The stonework is weathered, with visible textural variation and subtle discoloration, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The arches are evenly spaced and framed by decorative keystones and voussoirs, adding structural and visual interest. The parapet is solid and robust, providing both safety and a stylistic nod to historical architecture. The overall design is utilitarian yet ornate, suitable for pedestrian or light vehicular passage in an urban or park setting.
description: A weathered stone bridge with three arches and a crenellated parapet, featuring robust piers and decorative stonework. The structure combines utilitarian function with medieval-inspired architectural details.
description_view_0: Three arches and the crenellated parapet are clearly visible, showing the bridge's main span and decorative stonework.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the thickness of the parapet and the width of the bridge deck, with one supporting pier visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side of the bridge, mirroring the three arches and crenellated parapet, with similar weathered stone texture.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite side, again highlighting the parapet's thickness and the bridge's width.
attribute_railing_design: solid stone crenellated parapet with rectangular merlons and embrasures
